steps.title: Break the depth assumption
steps.content: The student's hypothesis relies on a general rule: shallower water is warmer because temperature decreases with depth. To weaken it, a finding must show that comparing depths across New Caledonia and Fiji is not enough to compare temperatures. A regional temperature difference could offset the expected effect of depth.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant findings
steps.content: Choice A concerns movement after hatching, not egg-laying temperature. Choice C explains why New Caledonian eggs might be shallower but does not weaken the warmth claim. Choice D would make Fijian water colder, strengthening the hypothesis. The answer is B.
